connections.gloss: along, away, because of, [phrase] by, conversation, custom, (east-) ward, journey, manner, passenger, through, toward, (high-) (path-) way(-side), whither(-soever).
cause to blow, bring, get, (make to) go (away, forth, forward, onward, out), (take) journey, march, remove, set aside (forward), [idiom] still, be on his (go their) way.

connections.def: apparently a primary word; a road; by implication, a progress (the route, act or distance); figuratively, a mode or means:--journey,

connections.gloss: accompany, bring (forward) on journey (way), conduct forth
connections.def: from 4253 and 3992; to send forward, i.e. escort or aid in travel:--accompany, bring (forward) on journey (way), conduct forth. see GREEK for 4253 see GREEK for

connections.gloss: --depart, go (away, forth, one's way, up), (make a, take a) journey, walk
connections.def: middle voice from a derivative of the same as 3984; to traverse, i.e. travel (literally or figuratively; especially to remove (figuratively, die), live, etc.); --depart, go (away, forth, one's way,…

connections.gloss: go (travel) into a far country, journey
connections.def: from 590; to go abroad, i.e. visit a foreign land:--go (travel) into a far country, journey. see GREEK for
